phrases.phrase: Display featured image on resource page by default
The default value of the per-resource "Display on page" toggle for newly created resources. Existing resources are not affected.
If enabled, resource pages emit an extended JSON-LD structured data block with featured image, ratings, downloads, and (when applicable) Product schema. Recommended for Google Rich Results.
When a resource's effective og:type is "product", change the JSON-LD @type to "Product" with offers (price + currency) and aggregateRating. Disable to keep the default CreativeWork schema.
Settings for the XFRM SEO Boost add-on (featured images, og:type defaults, JSON-LD output).
Override the <link rel="canonical"> URL. Leave blank to use the resource URL automatically. Use this only if the same content is intentionally available at multiple URLs.
If checked, the featured image is shown on the resource page (as a hero banner). The og:image meta tag for social sharing is always sent regardless of this setting.
The featured image must be a JPG, PNG, or WebP file.
The featured image is too large. Maximum size is {max_size}.
Upload a featured image used as og:image and shown on the resource card in listings. Recommended size: 1200x630px (Open Graph standard). Max 5 MB.
Recommended size: 1200×630 pixels (Open Graph standard). Max 5 MB. JPG, PNG, or WebP.
The primary keyword you want this resource to rank for. Used as guidance only; not output to HTML. Helps you stay focused while writing meta fields.
Override the auto-generated description used in <meta name="description"> and og:description. Leave blank to use a snippet of the resource description. Recommended length: 150-160 characters.
Override the page title shown to search engines and in social previews. Leave blank to use the resource title automatically. Recommended length: 50-60 characters.
